source.description: A six-panel raster marker contact sheet generated for the locked July 9 Doodlea.day current slug, then cropped into step frames and the finished master.
source.notes: The sheet must keep one face-free referee-style soccer whistle, with no text, numbers, labels, arrows, sticker border, or badge frame.

process_strategy: The whistle is built from the rounded body, mouthpiece, pea window, ring loop, motion/sound marks, color fills, and shadow before the final only thickens outlines and evens marker texture.

final_elements.name: rounded whistle body
final_elements.description: A chunky rounded whistle body tilted slightly upward.

final_elements.name: mouthpiece
final_elements.description: A short rectangular mouthpiece attached to one side.

final_elements.name: pea window
final_elements.description: A small oval or rounded cutout on the whistle body.

final_elements.name: ring loop
final_elements.description: A small loop at the back of the whistle for a lanyard.

final_elements.name: sound marks
final_elements.description: A few short comic sound arcs near the mouthpiece with no letters.

final_elements.name: yellow body fill
final_elements.description: Bright yellow marker fill on the whistle body.

final_elements.name: orange accents and blue shadow
final_elements.description: Orange marker accents on existing parts and a small blue ground shadow.

frames.visible_job: Blocks the main rounded whistle body at a slight angle.
Adds the short mouthpiece to the existing body.
Adds the small rounded pea window and inside edge on the whistle body.
Adds the rear ring loop and a few short sound arcs near the mouthpiece.
Adds yellow body fill, orange accents on existing pieces, and a small blue shadow.

final_step.visible_job: Only thickens the existing black outlines, evens the marker fills, clarifies the window, loop, sound marks, accents, and shadow.
allowed_changes: small highlight gaps on existing color areas

rejection_checks: No major contour, prop, marking, or color choice should first appear in the final image.
No frame may darken, ink, color, shade, or clean a feature that was not already visible in an earlier frame.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must keep the same pose, scale, and placement as the step frames.
